original_thesis: The 'think harder and better' stack. System 2 provides the horsepower; probabilistic thinking and expected value provide the framework; critical thinking provides the quality control; adverse selection warns about information asymmetry; deliberate practice builds the skill.
corrected_thesis: The 'see what others are hiding' stack. Adverse-selection warns that the information you're given may be strategically incomplete; causal-attribution-resistance prevents you from accepting the convenient explanation; critical thinking demands evidence; decomposition isolates the components; root-cause-analysis traces the real driver; scientific method tests the explanation. This stack defeats situations where information asymmetry is being exploited.
what_this_stack_defeats: Information asymmetry exploitation, convenient explanations, hidden adverse selection
notes: Original thesis referenced System 2, probabilistic thinking, expected value — none are members. Shares 5/6 with Causal Forensics trunk. Differentiator is adverse-selection, which shifts the stack toward asymmetric-information contexts (negotiations, markets, hiring). Part of the Causal Forensics supercluster.
